documents.title: Johns Hopkins University public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: Johns Hopkins has a research-facing AI stack including HopGPT, Advanced Research Computing, the Data Science and AI Institute, APL AI/autonomy work, Wilmer's AI center, and graduate AI programs.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Johns Hopkins University's public AI policy posture is comprehensive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Wilmer Artificial Intelligence Research Center
bodies.scope: Johns Hopkins has a research-facing AI stack including HopGPT, Advanced Research Computing, the Data Science and AI Institute, APL AI/autonomy work, Wilmer's AI center, and graduate AI programs.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.description: Johns Hopkins University has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.title: Generative AI teaching and assessment strategies
initiatives.description: Johns Hopkins AI in Education publishes teaching and assessment strategies for generative AI, including assignment design, expectations for AI use, and assessment approaches that reduce overreliance on AI detection.
initiatives.significance: The guidance adds an explicit assessment-redesign signal to Johns Hopkins' otherwise research-heavy AI profile.

investment.description: Public guidance does not disclose a separate assessment-redesign budget.

collaborations.description: The cited resource is institutional teaching guidance.

initiatives.policy_implications: The resource operationalizes AI policy through assignment and assessment design choices.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Johns Hopkins' Rockfish cluster publishes GPU-node counts: 18 four-GPU A100 40GB nodes, 10 four-GPU A100 80GB nodes, and 4 eight-GPU L40S nodes, for 144 GPUs total.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for MD. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Johns Hopkins University is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: Johns Hopkins has a research-facing AI stack including HopGPT, Advanced Research Computing, the Data Science and AI Institute, APL AI/autonomy work, Wilmer's AI center, and graduate AI programs. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Rockfish GPU capacity and HopGPT availability are public, but exact enterprise AI user counts, license terms, and contract values were not public.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: research compute powerhouse.
